steps.title: Recognize both functions are decreasing exponentials
steps.content:
Both $f$ and $g$ have base $0.3$, which satisfies $0 \lt 0.3 \lt 1$.

When the base of an exponential function is between 0 and 1, the function decreases as $x$ increases.

Since we want the maximum value where $x \geq 0$, the maximum occurs at $x = 0$.

We now need to check if the equation displays this maximum value as a constant or coefficient in its written form.

steps.title: Check statement I: evaluate $f(0)$
steps.content:
For $f(x) = 35(0.3)^{x+3}$, rewrite using the exponent rule $a^{m+n} = a^m \cdot a^n$:
$$f(x) = 35 \cdot (0.3)^3 \cdot (0.3)^x$$
Compute the constant factor:
$$(0.3)^3 = 0.027$$
$$35 \times 0.027 = 0.945$$
So the maximum value is $0.945$, but this number does not appear anywhere in the original equation $f(x) = 35(0.3)^{x+3}$. Statement I does not display the maximum as a constant or coefficient.

steps.title: Check statement II: simplify $g(x)$ using the substitution $0.09 = (0.3)^2$
steps.content:
For $g(x) = 35(0.09)(0.3)^{x-2}$, recognize that $0.09 = (0.3)^2$. Substitute and combine the exponents:
$$g(x) = 35 \cdot (0.3)^2 \cdot (0.3)^{x-2}$$
$$= 35 \cdot (0.3)^{2 + (x-2)}$$
$$= 35(0.3)^x$$
Now $g(0) = 35(0.3)^0 = 35$. The maximum value 35 appears explicitly as the coefficient in this rewritten form. Statement II does display the maximum as a constant or coefficient.

steps.title: Identify the correct answer
steps.content: Only statement II displays the maximum value as a constant or coefficient in its equation. That matches Choice B — II only.
